title: ChatGPT Work Arrives: OpenAI Ships an Agentic Workspace Two Days After Cowork Went Cross-Device
desc: OpenAI launched ChatGPT Work, a GPT-5.6 agentic workspace that plans, connects 1,400+ tools and ships docs, decks and dashboards. What operators should pilot.
keywords: chatgpt work, openai chatgpt work launch, chatgpt work vs claude cowork, agentic workspace, ai for business operations, chatgpt work plans, delegate work to ai
cta: Want an agentic workspace pilot scoped for your team?
body:
July 10, 2026. The other half of OpenAI's July 9 release was not a model. Alongside GPT-5.6's general availability, OpenAI launched ChatGPT Work, an agentic workspace that gathers context from a team's tools, plans an approach, and takes action across files, connected apps and the desktop to produce finished spreadsheets, documents, slides and dashboards. It is available to all plans on macOS desktop now and is rolling out to Windows desktop and to Plus, Pro, Business, Enterprise and Edu on web and mobile over the next few days. Two days earlier, Anthropic pushed Claude Cowork to web and mobile. The agentic work surface is now the front line of the platform war.

What ChatGPT Work ships

  1. Outcome-first workflows. Give it a goal and it gathers context, drafts a plan and executes across tools and files. In Plan mode it asks questions and waits for approval before work begins.
  2. Sites. Ideas, plans and data become shareable interactive dashboards, project trackers, calendars and web apps that stay current as information changes.
  3. 1,400+ plugins. Context flows in from the tools teams already use, and a built-in multi-tab browser in the desktop app supports richer agentic workflows across accounts.
  4. Scheduled and recurring tasks. One-time or recurring jobs run on a schedule, with progress checkable from a phone.
  5. The free path to GPT-5.6. Free and Go users get GPT-5.6 Terra inside ChatGPT Work, and the ultra multi-agent setting lives here for Pro and Enterprise plans.

OpenAI's customer stories put numbers on it, all vendor-reported: Zapier says a lead-triage system built in ChatGPT Work hands sales seven figures in pipeline monthly, an NVIDIA go-to-market manager automated roughly 40 percent of event number-crunching into a twice-weekly run, and Virgin Atlantic compressed a weeks-long competitor analysis cycle into hours.

What it means for operators

Anthropic's own usage data last week showed more than 90 percent of Cowork use is not coding, and OpenAI just aimed a product at exactly that work. The two launches, 48 hours apart, confirm the delegation surface is where the vendors will fight for your team, which means leverage for buyers who pilot deliberately. Pick one surface per team and run the same playbook we laid out for Cowork: inventory the work around the work, start with one scheduled task, put approval gates on anything that sends externally, and watch the meter, since agent runs on these surfaces bill in credits on some plans, as we covered when workspace agent pricing went live July 6. If you want the pilot scoped, instrumented and rolled out without burning a quarter on it, that is the exact work of our AI automation and AI automation agency teams.
faqs: What is ChatGPT Work and who can use it today?

ChatGPT Work is OpenAI's agentic workspace launched July 9 alongside GPT-5.6. It plans and executes work across connected tools, files and the desktop to produce finished documents, spreadsheets, slides and interactive Sites. It is available to all plans on macOS desktop now, with Windows desktop and web and mobile access for Plus, Pro, Business, Enterprise and Edu rolling out over the following days.
How is ChatGPT Work different from regular ChatGPT?

Regular ChatGPT answers in a conversation. ChatGPT Work is outcome-first: it gathers context from more than 1,400 plugins, proposes a plan you approve, acts across tools and a built-in browser, runs scheduled and recurring tasks, and delivers share-ready artifacts rather than chat replies.
How does ChatGPT Work compare to Claude Cowork?

They are converging on the same shape: cross-device agentic workspaces for business operations, launched within 48 hours of each other. Cowork leads with background sessions, offline scheduled tasks and phone approvals; ChatGPT Work counters with Plan mode, Sites, a larger connector catalog and free-tier access to GPT-5.6 Terra. Most teams should pilot one surface deliberately rather than adopt both at once.

Access comes with existing ChatGPT plans during the rollout, and Free and Go users get GPT-5.6 Terra inside it. Heavier agentic usage is where costs appear: the ultra multi-agent setting is limited to Pro and Enterprise in ChatGPT Work, and workspace agent runs on business plans bill in credits under the pricing OpenAI switched on July 6.
